description: Requirements to wield: class, age, gender, time, location, and context gates that determine who can use the item and when.
limitations: Physician's Instrument
Only a healer's trained hands can coax this item to life. It knows the touch and refuses all others.
Warrior's Grip
The item answers only to those who have bled in battle. Soft hands find it cold and lifeless.
Arcane Vessel
Raw magic must flow through the wielder's veins. Without it, the item is nothing more than dead weight.
Naturalist's Bond
It responds to those who speak the old tongue of root and rain. All others hear only silence.
Ranger's Companion
The wild places have to live inside you first. This item can tell the difference between wanderer and woodsman.
Spy's Tool
It hides from honest hands. Only those practiced in shadow and deception can feel it stir.
Wizard's Focus
Deep study and careful learning are the only keys. The item tests knowledge, not talent, and finds most wanting.
Tyrant's Scepter
Power recognizes power. This item bows only to those who command others, and goes silent for the rest.
Commoner's Charm
Crowns and titles make it recoil. It was made for simple folk, and only simple folk may use it.
Servant's Burden
It belongs to the overlooked and the obedient. Those who give orders find it impossibly stubborn.
Invoker's Channel
The wielder must be a living conduit for higher forces. Without that divine current, the item stays dormant.
Respite Only
It stirs when the fighting stops. In the calm between dangers, it finally agrees to cooperate.
Daybound
Sunlight is the key. When the sun climbs, the item wakes. When it sets, the power drains like water.
Nightbound
Moonlight and starlight feed it. Dawn kills it. Every sunrise is a small death for this thing.
Battle Hungry
It lives for the clash of arms. Outside of a fight, it refuses to so much as glow.
Peacewarden
Violence makes it recoil. It functions only when no blows are being struck nearby.
Sanctuary Sealed
Only within safe walls does it consent to work. Danger makes it lock tight.
Mindkeeper
It demands all of your attention. Break focus for even a moment and its power slips away like sand.
Afternoon Waker
The morning means nothing to it. Only after the sun crosses its peak does the item begin to stir.
Morning Fader
It rises with the dawn and fades by noon. By afternoon, you are carrying an ornament.
Hearthbound
Open sky offends it. Walls and a roof overhead are the only conditions it accepts.
Wanderer's Relic
Ceilings smother its power. It needs wind, sky, and open earth to breathe.
Elder's Right
Youth cannot wake it. Only those who have lived long enough carry the weight it recognizes.
Child's Treasure
It dims at the first sign of adulthood. Only young hands and young hearts make it sing.
Father's Oath
It answers to the bond of fatherhood. Those without children of their own find it cold and mute.
Maiden's Grace
It responds only to women. A man's grip earns nothing but silence.
Lord's Favor
It answers only to men. A woman's touch leaves it inert.
Mother's Vigil
It knows a mother's hands. The fierce tenderness of that bond is the only key that fits.
Lunar Zenith
Only under the full moon's unbroken gaze does it wake. Every other night, it sleeps like stone.
Wicked Purpose
It thrives on cruelty and selfish ambition. Noble intentions make it go dead.
Virtuous Key
Only pure intent unlocks it. The slightest selfish thought and the power withdraws.
Shadow Strike
Unseen hands are the only ones it obeys. Step into the light, and it becomes useless.
Riposte Only
It waits for the enemy to swing first. No first strikes. Only answers.
Sun Forged
Direct sunlight must touch it or it sleeps. Clouds, shade, and rooftops all silence it.
Morning Star
It belongs to the early hours, when dew still clings. By midday, its fire has gone cold.
Evening Star
It waits for the long shadows. Only when the sky turns amber does the power stir.
Veil of Darkness
Total darkness is its element. Even a candle flame makes it flinch and go quiet.
Dawn Rite
Once at dawn, and only at dawn. The item gives everything it has in a single moment, then rests until tomorrow.
Innocent Hand
Blood on your hands today means the item locks shut. It opens only for those who have spared every life since sunrise.
Even Resonance
It vibrates in sympathy with a peculiar balance. Only certain creatures, those with an even inner harmony, feel it respond.
Truthkeeper
One lie within the hour and the item knows. It locks down until it trusts your tongue again.
Agoraphobe
It hates the open sky and refuses to work beneath it. Bring it indoors or carry a paperweight.
Claustrophobe
Walls close in and the item shuts down. It needs open sky overhead to function.
Bare Skin Ban
Armor smothers it. The wielder must stand unshielded for the item to recognize their courage.
Current's Bane
Running water is an impassable wall. The item drags you to a halt at every stream and river.
Threshold Locked
A closed door means nothing, but a spoken welcome means everything. Without invitation, it will not cross.
Waning Crescent
It wakes only under a dying moon at night. A narrow window of power that demands patience.
Desperate Edge
The weaker you are, the louder it sings. At full strength, it barely whispers.
Earthwalker
Bare feet on living soil. That is the only circuit that completes the item's power.
Fearbright
Terror is the fuel. Only when the wielder's hands are shaking does the item blaze to life.
Hallowed Exile
Holy ground burns through the wrapping. The item forces its bearer away from sacred places.
Hydrophobe
Water kills it. Submerge it or soak it and the magic drowns, leaving dead material behind.
Kin Shield
It refuses to harm the wielder's own people. Against your own blood, it is nothing but dead weight.
Passing Hands
It grows restless in the same grip. A new bearer each cycle, or the power begins to fade.
Shifting Pact
It craves variety in its masters. Each week demands a different kind of soul to keep it fed.
Incomplete Set
Alone it is inert. Its siblings must be near, a matched collection, or the magic stays locked inside.
Dawn Waker
The first light of day is its signal. Only at dawn does it agree to stir.
Dusk Waker
It waits for the dying light. Only as the sun sinks does the item come alive.
Ether Touch
Solid things bore it. Only the magical and the ghostly catch its interest.
Shared Grasp
It demands community. Multiple hands must hold it at once before it will respond.
Blood Claim
Ownership passes only through violence. You earn it by ending the last one who held it.
Duelist's Prize
It changes hands only in formal contest. Theft and trade mean nothing to it.
Midnight Bloom
It flowers at the stroke of midnight and wilts by one. A fleeting window of absolute power.
Deepcaller
Sunlight poisons its magic. Only below the surface, surrounded by stone and root, does it function.
Elder Only
It reads the years in your bones. Only the old, with their weight of memory, can wake it.
Maker's Kin
It remembers who forged it. Only those who share the maker's blood can draw out its power.
Crown's Demand
Noble blood is the only key. Common hands find it locked and lifeless.
Oral Tradition
The secret of its use passes only from teacher to student. Without that chain, you hold a mystery.
Living History
The wielder must know every chapter of the item's past. Without that knowledge, it stays asleep.
Homeland's Reach
Carry it too far from where it was made and the magic thins like stretched thread until it snaps.
One and Done
It gives itself to a single wielder, fully and without reservation. When that bond is spent, it is spent forever.
Depthborn
It belongs to the deep water. Only fully submerged does it stir, useless in the dry air above.
Windrunner
Stillness kills its power. The wielder must be in motion, always moving, for the magic to flow.
Killing Edge
It does not understand restraint. Every wound it delivers is meant to be the last.
Merciful Blow
It refuses to take a life. At the final moment, the power pulls back, leaving victims broken but breathing.
Bare Ward
Skin contact is lethal. The wielder must always use gloves, cloth, or sheath between flesh and surface.
Deepward
Below the surface it refuses to wake. It was made for the sky and the wind, not stone corridors.
Peasant's Claim
Titles and lineage make it recoil. It serves only those who have earned nothing by birth.
Drowned Relic
It was never meant for water. Submerge it and the enchantment unravels like wet thread.
Open Secret
Those who know its story become immune to it. The more famous it grows, the less effective it becomes.
Expert's Puzzle
Its abilities are locked behind layers of complexity. Only a scholar's careful examination reveals how it works.
Missing Half
It is one piece of a greater whole. Without its partner nearby, it sits inert and waiting.
Two Handed
It demands both hands and full commitment. There is no room for a shield or a second weapon.
Rooted Stance
Using it pins the wielder in place. Feet lock to the ground until the effect ends.
Mastery Gate
It tests for a specific expertise and finds most people lacking. Without that rare skill, it is ornamental.
Sworn Bearer
An oath must be spoken before it will answer. The words bind the wielder as much as the item.
